figures.caption: Figure 13: Training loss and accuracy on MNIST. Fixed parameters: d = 25 450, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Columns vary the number of workers n ∈ {10, 100, 1000}; rows alternate between loss and accuracy for the per-sample computation time h ∈ {0, 0.1, 1.0}.

Figure 8: Convergence for medium heterogeneity (ℓ = 0.3) and low noise σ = 0.001. Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.

Figure 9: Convergence for high heterogeneity (ℓ = 0.5) and low noise σ = 0.001. Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.

Figure 10: Convergence for medium heterogeneity (ℓ = 0.3) and medium noise σ = 0.01. Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.

Figure 11: Convergence for high heterogeneity (ℓ = 0.5) and medium noise σ = 0.01. Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.
Figure 4: Hessian ratio, loss, and accuracy during training for step size = 0.01 and δ = 10.
Figure 3: Hessian ratio, loss, and accuracy during training for step size = 0.01 and δ = 2.
Figure 1: Hessian ratio, loss, and accuracy during training for step size = 0.01.
Figure 2: Hessian ratio, loss, and accuracy during training for step size = 0.001.

Figure 5: Convergence under low gradient noise (σ = 0.001). Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.

Figure 12: Training loss and accuracy on homogeneous MNIST. Columns correspond to per-sample computation time h ∈ {0, 0.1, 1.0}. Rows (top to bottom): n = 10 (loss), n = 10 (accuracy), n = 100 (loss), n = 100 (accuracy). Fixed parameters: d = 25 450, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d.

Figure 6: Convergence under medium gradient noise (σ = 0.01). Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.

Figure 7: Convergence under high gradient noise (σ = 0.1). Fixed parameters: d = 300, κ = 1/d, τ = 1/d. Rows vary the gradient computation time h; columns correspond to the number of workers n ∈ {50, 100, 300}.
